name: GSD
description: A lightweight meta-prompting, context-engineering and spec-driven development system that wraps Claude Code and 14+ other AI coding runtimes with a discuss → plan → execute → verify workflow.

description.value: Lightweight meta-prompting, context-engineering and spec-driven development system for Claude Code and other AI coding runtimes, built around a discuss → plan → execute → verify loop with parallel agent orchestration.

website.comment: No dedicated marketing site; README's `homepage` in package.json points to the GitHub repo.

creator.comment: Signed README author; package.json `author` field. Solo-developer branded project.

pricing-model.comment: MIT OSS. No paid tier for the workflow itself; underlying AI runtimes have their own pricing.

covered-phases.comment: Discovery via /gsd-map-codebase, spec via /gsd-new-project questions → REQUIREMENTS.md, architecture via research agents, planning via /gsd-plan-phase + ROADMAP.md, atomic task plans, /gsd-execute-phase for implementation, /gsd-review + code-reviewer agent, /gsd-verify-work UAT for testing, /gsd-milestone-summary + /gsd-forensics for retrospective. No deployment phase — /gsd-ship only creates PRs.

methodology-style.comment: Self-described as spec-driven development system; plan-and-execute via discuss→plan→execute; multi-agent because the orchestrator spawns specialized subagents (researchers, planner, checker, executors, verifier, debuggers); harness-centric since the value is a curated set of commands, agents, hooks and prompts layered over an existing AI runtime.

opinionated.comment: Prescribes a fixed workflow (discuss → plan → execute → verify → ship) with phase-boundary hook enforcement and strict artifact layout under .planning/.

ceremony-level.comment: Self-described as lightweight ("no enterprise roleplay bullshit") — fewer artifacts than BMAD or Agent-OS, but still enforces multiple mandatory files per phase (PROJECT.md, REQUIREMENTS.md, ROADMAP.md, STATE.md, CONTEXT.md, RESEARCH.md, PLAN.md, SUMMARY.md, UAT.md, VERIFICATION.md) so not low.

mechanization-level.comment: Single slash-command invokes full end-to-end pipelines (e.g. /gsd-execute-phase runs parallel waves with atomic commits). /gsd-next auto-detects next step; --auto and --chain flags chain phases without stopping. Not very-high because user invokes each phase boundary command by default.

hitl-checkpoints.comment: Default `mode: interactive` confirms at each step; explicit user approvals for roadmap, discuss decisions, plans, and UAT.

approval-gates.comment: User approves the roadmap/requirements after /gsd-new-project (spec gate), approves plans before /gsd-execute-phase (plan gate), and performs UAT via /gsd-verify-work after execution (implementation gate).

autonomous-e2e.comment: `mode: yolo`, `--auto`, `--chain`, `workflow.auto_advance`, and /gsd-autonomous enable end-to-end runs without pausing. README recommends running Claude Code with --dangerously-skip-permissions for frictionless automation.

autonomy-level.comment: Default is phase-level interactive approvals (≈3), but autonomous switches (yolo mode, --chain, auto_advance) are first-class and the README encourages frictionless automation, pushing the overall default-experience rating toward 4.

rollback-support.comment: Atomic per-task commits are an explicit design goal ("Each task independently revertable") so `git revert` on any task commit is the built-in rollback. No bespoke undo command beyond git.

supported-agents.comment: Installer supports Claude Code, OpenCode, Gemini CLI, Kilo, Codex, Copilot, Cursor, Windsurf, Antigravity, Augment, Trae, Qwen Code, CodeBuddy, Cline. Gemini/Kilo/Antigravity/Augment/Trae/Qwen/CodeBuddy map to `other` since no dedicated tag exists. No Aider support documented.

editor-integrations.comment: VS Code family via Copilot Chat, Cursor, Windsurf, Cline, Augment, Trae, CodeBuddy. Terminal CLIs: Claude Code, OpenCode, Gemini CLI, Codex, Qwen Code. No JetBrains or Neovim-specific integration documented.

model-agnostic.comment: Supports 14+ runtimes with different underlying models. `inherit` model profile explicitly delegates model choice to the runtime, usable with OpenRouter/local models. Default profiles assume Claude (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) but are configurable.

mcp-support.comment: Research and UI agents declare MCP tools in frontmatter (mcp__context7__*, mcp__firecrawl__*, mcp__exa__*, Playwright-MCP). GSD consumes MCP for docs lookup, web crawling, and browser automation; it does not itself expose an MCP server.

artifact-formats.comment: Markdown for all human-readable artifacts (PROJECT.md, ROADMAP.md, PLAN.md, etc.). Plans embed XML-structured task blocks. JSON for `.planning/config.json`, `HANDOFF.json`, `INVENTORY-MANIFEST.json`.

artifacts-covered.comment: user-docs via /gsd-docs-update; architecture via project/phase researchers + .planning/research/; ROADMAP.md; REQUIREMENTS.md + CONTEXT.md as specs; PLAN.md; atomic task blocks inside plans; milestone summaries and tagged releases cover release-notes. Decisions/ADRs not a first-class artifact; test-plans covered only informally via verifier, not a distinct artifact type.

project-layout.value: .planning/ directory at repo root holding PROJECT.md, REQUIREMENTS.md, ROADMAP.md, STATE.md, config.json, plus per-phase subdirs with CONTEXT.md, RESEARCH.md, PLAN.md, SUMMARY.md, UAT.md, VERIFICATION.md; commands/agents/hooks install under runtime-specific roots (.claude/, .codex/, .opencode/, etc.).

artifacts-in-git.comment: `planning.commit_docs` defaults to true; atomic commits per task explicitly include .planning/ changes. /gsd-pr-branch filters planning commits when creating clean PR branches.

language-agnostic.comment: Meta-prompting layer with no language-specific tooling; research and planner agents adapt to any stack. GSD itself is packaged as npm (Node ≥22) but operates on any project language.

enforcement-mechanisms.comment: Hooks directory ships phase-boundary, workflow-guard, prompt-guard, validate-commit, context-monitor, read-guard, injection-scanner. Agent-review via plan-checker, verifier, code-reviewer, security-auditor subagents. Manual approval at phase transitions. CI runs prompt-injection-scan and agent size-budget checks. Instructional since commands/agents are markdown prompts.

strict-phase-ordering.comment: `gsd-phase-boundary.sh` hook enforces phase transitions; /gsd-health and workflow-guard detect out-of-order state. Phases follow discuss → plan → execute → verify.

artifact-validation.comment: /gsd-health validates .planning/ integrity with --repair; plan-checker verifies plans against requirements; prompt-injection-scan.test.cjs validates agent/command files; XML task structure enforced by planner/executor prompts.

memory-implementation.comment: Persistent context stored as STATE.md (decisions/blockers/position), PROJECT.md (vision), threads/ (cross-session), seeds/ (forward-looking), todos/, .planning/research/.

persistent-memory.comment: STATE.md explicitly "memory across sessions"; /gsd-pause-work writes HANDOFF.json, /gsd-resume-work restores; threads persist cross-session knowledge.

project-context-files.comment: PROJECT.md is the always-loaded project context file. Runtime-specific context files also produced (.clinerules for Cline, etc.).

cost-tracking.comment: No token or dollar-cost tracking. gsd-context-monitor hook shows context-window usage warnings (not token cost). Model profiles (quality/balanced/budget) are the only spend control.

run-history.comment: Per-task SUMMARY.md, per-phase VERIFICATION.md and UAT.md, HANDOFF.json handoffs, /gsd-session-report, /gsd-forensics post-mortem all contribute structured history in .planning/.

telemetry-opt.comment: No telemetry collected back to GSD — it is entirely local. SDK README mentions "event-stream telemetry" but that is in-process observability for agents, not usage reporting. Attribute treated as not applicable.

custom-templates.comment: agent_skills.<agent_type> in config.json injects project-specific skill directories into subagents at spawn. /gsd-spike-wrap-up and /gsd-sketch-wrap-up package findings into project-local skills. Agent prompts themselves are markdown files users can override post-install.

hooks.comment: Hooks dir ships PreToolUse/PostToolUse-style hooks (gsd-prompt-guard, gsd-workflow-guard, gsd-validate-commit, gsd-phase-boundary, gsd-context-monitor, gsd-read-guard, gsd-read-injection-scanner, gsd-statusline). Users can extend via the underlying runtime's hook system (e.g. Claude Code settings.json) since GSD hooks are regular scripts.

sub-agents.comment: 30+ built-in specialized agents (gsd-planner, gsd-executor, gsd-verifier, gsd-debugger, gsd-security-auditor, gsd-ui-auditor, etc.). Users inject custom skills via agent_skills config and package project-local skills via /gsd-spike-wrap-up.

marketplace.comment: No official template or skill marketplace. Community ports (gsd-opencode, gsd-gemini) exist but are adaptations, not a template registry.

install-mechanism.comment: Distributed as `get-shit-done-cc` on npm; primary install is `npx get-shit-done-cc@latest` which runs an interactive installer. Source install from the cloned repo is documented but secondary.

setup-time.comment: Single `npx get-shit-done-cc@latest` command with interactive prompts for runtime and location; non-interactive flags available for Docker/CI.

self-hosted.comment: All execution is local; no GSD-hosted service. Underlying AI runtime (Claude API, OpenAI, etc.) is external, but GSD itself runs entirely on the user's machine.

requires-account.comment: No GSD account. Requires a working AI runtime (which itself may need a vendor account) but that is separate from the workflow.

git-worktree-support.comment: `workflow.use_worktrees` defaults true for execution isolation. /gsd-new-workspace creates isolated workspaces via worktrees (or clones as fallback). Workstreams use worktrees for parallel milestone work.

documentation-quality.comment: Extensive README, dedicated USER-GUIDE.md, AGENTS.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, COMMANDS.md, CONFIGURATION.md, FEATURES.md, CLI-TOOLS.md, INVENTORY.md, workflow-discuss-mode.md, manual-update.md; README translated into pt-BR, zh-CN, ja-JP, ko-KR; per-agent markdown files document responsibilities. Documentation is thorough, current, and example-rich.

github-stars.comment: 55.6k stars captured 2026-04-21 via `gh repo view`. Stars rose rapidly from repo creation on 2025-12-14; number stales quickly.

active-maintenance.comment: Last push 2026-04-21 (today); v1.38.1 released 2026-04-19; weekly release cadence. Well within the 90-day threshold.

example-projects.comment: README cites testimonials ("Trusted by engineers at Amazon, Google, Shopify, Webflow") but no linked example repositories or documented case studies. Community ports exist but are ports, not usage examples.

community-channel.comment: Official Discord server linked prominently in README (invite https://discord.gg/mYgfVNfA2r). X/Twitter account @gsd_foundation exists for announcements but is not a community discussion channel.
